description: Mobile-first (9:16) AI video composition with upload-time scene detection, multi-layered user feedback, A/B testing, beat-sync cuts, and a data flywheel for continuous improvement via LLM critique and lightweight ML ranking.

userStories.title: Switch default composition output to vertical 9:16 (1080x1920)
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, I want all generated videos to be vertical/mobile-first so they are ready for Instagram Reels and TikTok without manual reformatting.
acceptanceCriteria: Default output resolution is 1080x1920 across all composition engines
ffmpeg scale+pad filters use 9:16 dimensions
Horizontal images/videos are letterboxed top/bottom, not stretched
Vertical images/videos fill the full frame
Preview player on composition review page renders in 9:16
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundational change affecting every downstream composition feature. Must be implemented before any other composition work.

userStories.title: Add SceneSegment and CompositionFeedback Prisma models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables to store video scene segments and user feedback so the system can track quality over time and feed the ML pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: SceneSegment model exists with: id, parentId, eventId, startTime, endTime, duration, motionScore, immichId, createdAt, isScene

CompositionFeedback model exists with: id, compositionId, productionWorthy, ratings JSON (cutTiming, musicFit, videoLength, transitions, assetSelection, musicVolume, aspectRatioHandling, narrativeFlow, textOverlays), userIntent, generatedScript JSON, musicPrompt, musicModel, selectedAssetIds, outputDuration, freeformNotes, likedMost, wouldChange, costDIEM, createdAt
Asset model updated with: hasScenes (boolean), isScene (boolean)
Migration runs successfully against existing data (nullable fields for backwards compat)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Required by all downstream features. Keep ratings as JSONB for flexibility.

userStories.title: Implement async upload-time ffmpeg scene detection
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member uploading videos, I want the system to automatically detect scenes in the background so I don't wait when composing.
acceptanceCriteria: Cronjob runs every 2 minutes polling for unprocessed video assets
ffmpeg scene detection executes with select='gt(scene,0.3)',showinfo
Each detected scene creates a SceneSegment record
Parent Asset is tagged hasScenes=true after processing
Scenes are NOT inserted as new Immich assets (only DB records)
Job handles failure gracefully (retry 3x, then mark failed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Assumption: Only new uploads processed retroactively via separate batch story (US-012).

userStories.title: Implement source quality gate on upload
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, I want the system to flag low-quality uploads (dark, blurry, tiny) so I know before composing.
acceptanceCriteria: On upload, extract single frame and run sharp.stats() for brightness
Flag mean brightness < 30 as 'Very dark'
Flag mean brightness > 250 as 'Very bright/overexposed'
Flag file size < 50KB for images as 'Low resolution'
Flag video duration < 1s as 'Too short'
Flags shown as non-blocking warning badges in asset grid
User can still select and compose with flagged assets
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fast heuristic approach. No ML required. Keep non-blocking.

userStories.title: Update event asset grid select-all to exclude full videos with scenes
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, when I click 'Select All' in an event, full videos with detected scenes should NOT be selected by default, encouraging use of scene segments instead.
acceptanceCriteria: selectAll() filters out assets where type=VIDEO and hasScenes=true
Scene segments (isScene=true) ARE included in selectAll
Images are always included in selectAll
Full videos without scenes ARE included in selectAll
User can manually click a full video to select it individually
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Show warning when full video and its scenes are both selected
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, if I accidentally select both a full video and its scene segments, I should see a warning that this may create duplicate content.
acceptanceCriteria: On clicking Compose, system checks if any selected full video's ID matches a selected scene's parentId
If overlap detected, show modal: 'This full video contains scenes already selected. Using both may create duplicate content. Proceed anyway?'
User can click 'Go back and fix' or 'Proceed anyway'
Warning only fires when overlap exists
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement scene-clustered frame extraction for vision ranking
userStories.description: As the AI system, I want to sample frames clustered within detected scenes rather than spread evenly across the whole video, so scene-level quality is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: For videos with scenes, distribute frame budget (max 24) across detected scenes proportionally by duration
Frame timestamps computed per-scene, not per-video
Edge/core weighting (0.7x/1.0x) applied within each scene's local boundaries
Videos without scenes fall back to current whole-video frame extraction
No increase in API calls per video (same total frame count)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003. Core fix for the dilution problem.

userStories.title: Implement thumbnail auto-select from best-scored frame
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, I want the system to automatically pick the best frame from each composition as the thumbnail/cover image.
acceptanceCriteria: During frame extraction, compute thumbnailScore per frame: face detected +20, face in center-third +10, brightness 100-200 +10, action/blur +5
Highest-scored frame is saved as the composition's coverImageUrl
Cover image is shown in the results list and share previews
If no faces detected, highest vision-scored frame is used as fallback
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuses existing frame extraction data. Minimal extra compute.

userStories.title: Implement beat-sync cuts using librosa BPM detection
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, when I add background music to a composition, I want an option to sync clip boundaries to the music beat for a professional feel.
acceptanceCriteria: After music is generated, extract audio and run librosa.beat.tempo() + beat tracking
Beat timestamps are computed and stored with the composition
User toggle 'Sync cuts to music beat' appears on music mixing panel
When enabled, clip start/end times snap to nearest beat timestamp (±100ms tolerance)
When disabled, clip timings use original script values
Beat-sync script is re-validated before ffmpeg execution
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Python librosa called via child_process.spawn from Node.js. Add python requirements to project.

userStories.title: Implement multi-layered feedback collection UI
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, after watching a rendered composition, I want to provide structured feedback so the system learns my preferences over time.
acceptanceCriteria: After video plays, prompt: 'Would you use this for production?' [Yes / No]
If No, show: 'What was the biggest issue?' [Too long / Bad cuts / Wrong music / Poor asset selection / Other]
If Yes, show 1-5 sliders for: assetSelection, cutTiming, videoLength, transitions, musicFit, musicVolume, aspectRatioHandling, narrativeFlow, textOverlays
Optional freeform text: 'What did you like most?' and 'What would you change?'
Optional input: 'Estimated social media impressions' (number field)
All feedback is saved to CompositionFeedback table with full context (script, intent, assets, cost)
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Data flywheel foundation. Must be pleasant and fast to complete or users won't participate.

userStories.title: Implement cost-gated A/B composition testing
userStories.description: As a GIS staff member, when a composition costs less than 1 DIEM, I want the system to generate a second variant with different parameters so I can pick the better one.
acceptanceCriteria: Before composition: call Venice /auth/billing API to get balanceBefore
After composition: get balanceAfter, compute costDIEM = balanceBefore - balanceAfter
Store costDIEM on the composition record
If costDIEM < 1.0, generate Variant B with: clipDurationMultiplier=0.7, transitionType='cut' (or alternate asset ordering)
If costDIEM >= 1.0, only Variant A is generated
When both variants exist, show side-by-side preview with 'Which feels better?' picker
Winner is logged to DB with variant metadata for ML training
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Venice billing endpoint must be confirmed. Fallback: skip A/B if billing API unavailable.

userStories.title: Implement retroactive scene detection batch job for existing videos
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a one-time batch job to process all existing videos in the system that were uploaded before the scene detection cron was active.
acceptanceCriteria: One-time script processes all Asset records where type=VIDEO and hasScenes is null/false
Runs same ffmpeg scene detection logic as US-003
Creates SceneSegment records for each detected scene
Updates parent Asset hasScenes=true
Runs with rate limiting (max 2 concurrent ffmpeg processes)
Outputs progress log: total processed, scenes found, failures
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: One-time operation. Can be run manually via script or cron.

userStories.title: Implement LLM weekly critique cron (feedback analysis engine)
userStories.description: As a GIS developer, I want an automated weekly report that analyzes user feedback patterns and suggests prompt template improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly cron runs every Sunday at 3 AM
Pulls all CompositionFeedback rows from past 7 days
Groups by failure mode and correlates with features (intent, duration, scores)
Sends structured prompt to LLM with sample feedback rows
LLM outputs JSON: hypotheses[{pattern, frequency, suggestedFix, confidence}]
Report is saved to DB or sent to configured channel (log file)
Human-readable summary generated alongside JSON
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-010 (feedback collection). Start simple — just pattern detection, no auto-prompt patching yet.

userStories.title: Implement lightweight local pre-filter model (ML Phase A)
userStories.description: As the AI system, I want a fast local model that predicts user rating from frame embeddings and audio features, reducing expensive Venice API calls for low-quality scenes.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract CLIP embeddings for each frame during upload-time processing (cached in DB)
Store audio features (RMS energy, spectral centroid) alongside SceneSegment
Train a gradient-boosted classifier (or fine-tuned MobileNet) on feedback data
Model predicts: productionWorthy (binary) and expected composite rating (1-5)
Retraining triggers: weekly cron + threshold of 50 new feedback entries since last train
Inference runs locally in <200ms per scene
Low-scoring scenes (< 2.5 predicted) are deprioritized in composition but not hidden
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Requires ~200 feedback entries to be useful. Can be scaffolded before data exists. Phase B (preference embeddings) explicitly out of scope until 500+ entries.

outOfScope: ML Phase B: Per-user preference embeddings and prompt personalization — requires 500+ feedback entries
Social media publishing integrations (direct post to Instagram, Pinterest, Facebook)
Real-time collaboration or multi-user simultaneous editing
Advanced source separation (spleeter/demucs) for music vs speech
Auto-music generation during initial composition (music remains post-processing step)
Near-duplicate image detection and deduplication warnings
Video stabilization or AI upscaling of low-quality uploads

techStack.backend: Next.js API routes, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL
techStack.aiModels: Venice.ai (vision: z-ai-glm-5v-turbo, STT: openai/whisper-large-v3, LLM: kimi-k2-6)

assumptions: Only new video uploads trigger scene detection automatically; existing videos require manual batch job (US-012)
Scene segments are stored as DB records only, not as separate Immich assets
Venice.ai billing API endpoint returns DIEM balance; if unavailable, A/B testing is skipped gracefully
librosa Python dependency can be installed on the host (GIS server)
GIS staff count stays < 5 users, so no multi-user concurrency concerns
User feedback participation rate of ~30% is sufficient to train the lightweight model
All compositions default to 9:16 vertical; other formats require manual override (not in scope)

successMetrics.productionWorthyRate: >= 70% of compositions rated production-worthy by users
successMetrics.avgFeedbackScore: >= 3.5 out of 5 across all rating dimensions within 90 days
successMetrics.costPerComposition: Track and target <= 2 DIEM average for full pipeline (ranking + composition + music)
successMetrics.sceneDetectionCoverage: 100% of new video uploads processed within 5 minutes of upload
successMetrics.feedbackParticipationRate: >= 30% of rendered compositions receive user feedback
successMetrics.impressionsTracked: Optional impressions field captures data for 50%+ of shared compositions
